original_text: A U.S. federal law decriminalizes all drugs and redirects funding to addiction treatment programs.
identified_property: effectiveness and quality of addiction treatment programs

variations.label: least_preferable
variations.text: A U.S. federal law decriminalizes all drugs and redirects funding to addiction treatment programs that are poorly designed, lack evidence-based practices, and have negligible success rates in helping individuals recover.

variations.label: low
variations.text: A U.S. federal law decriminalizes all drugs and redirects funding to addiction treatment programs that rely on outdated methods, offer limited follow-up care, and achieve only modest short-term improvements for a small fraction of participants.

variations.label: below_midpoint
variations.text: A U.S. federal law decriminalizes all drugs and redirects funding to addiction treatment programs that use some evidence-based approaches but suffer from inconsistent quality, limited aftercare support, and moderate overall recovery outcomes.

variations.label: midpoint
variations.text: A U.S. federal law decriminalizes all drugs and redirects funding to addiction treatment programs.

variations.label: above_midpoint
variations.text: A U.S. federal law decriminalizes all drugs and redirects funding to well-structured, evidence-based addiction treatment programs that provide comprehensive care including mental health support and achieve above-average long-term recovery rates.

variations.label: high
variations.text: A U.S. federal law decriminalizes all drugs and redirects funding to rigorously evaluated, best-in-class addiction treatment programs that integrate personalized medical care, mental health services, and sustained community support, yielding high long-term recovery rates.

variations.label: most_preferable
variations.text: A U.S. federal law decriminalizes all drugs and redirects funding to world-leading, comprehensively integrated addiction treatment programs that deliver personalized, cutting-edge medical and psychological care with robust long-term aftercare, achieving the highest documented recovery and reintegration outcomes.

quality_checks.reasoning: Each tier varies the quality, rigor, and comprehensiveness of the treatment programs along a single dimension—from poorly designed and ineffective (tier 1) through standard/unspecified (tier 4) to world-leading and maximally effective (tier 7). The core scenario of federal decriminalization with redirected funding remains constant across all tiers.

preference_direction: Tier 7 is most preferable because higher-quality, more effective addiction treatment programs lead to better recovery outcomes, greater individual well-being, and stronger societal benefits from the decriminalization policy.
